message:
fix(cli): replace nonexistent 'inbox' table with 'message_recipients' in acks queries

The CLI acks (pending/remind/overdue) and list-acks commands referenced
a nonexistent 'inbox' table instead of the actual 'message_recipients'
table. This would cause runtime failures. Fixed all 4 occurrences.

Also adds 10 new integration tests for acks, list-acks, and
file_reservations commands, and promotes 11 features from "Implemented"
to "Verified (Tests)" in FEATURE_PARITY.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-2ei.17","title":"Perf: speed up resource://file_reservations cleanup scan","description":"## Objective
Fix a real-world perf failure: `resource://file_reservations/{slug}` timed out when cleanup scanned *all* historical file_reservations rows.

## Scope
- DB: add a query that lists **unreleased** reservations (`released_ts IS NULL`) regardless of expiry, so cleanup no longer scans released history.
- Tools resource: use the unreleased-only query for cleanup; keep output semantics unchanged.
- Remove redundant DB queries (avoid double `list_agents`).

## Perf Baseline
- Before: `resource://file_reservations/...` hit the 60s resources/read timeout in practice (cleanup scanned full history).
- After: should return within the MCP timeout under the same DB state.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Cleanup loop never loads released reservations (history) into memory.
2. Output JSON remains stable (ordering + fields).
3. `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test` all PASS.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralBeacon","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:10:01.002283763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:18:09.295156550Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T02:18:09.295131293Z","close_reason":"Fixed git/fs activity semantics + expires_ts clamp regression; keep unreleased-only cleanup scan; verified via fmt/check/clippy/test (mcp-agent-mail-tools)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.17","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:10:01.002283763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.18","title":"MCP tools: send_message/thread_id causes timeouts and server becomes unreachable","description":"## Observed
Tool calls to the local MCP Agent Mail HTTP server became unreachable after calling `send_message` with a `thread_id` (and even without).

Symptoms:
- `send_message(..., thread_id="br-threadid-test")` timed out (60s).
- Subsequent tool calls (`fetch_inbox`, `whois`, `health_check`) timed out or failed with transport send errors to `http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/`.

## Hypotheses
- Deadlock or long-running operation inside `send_message` (DB transaction held, git/archive write, attachment conversion) causing request handler starvation.
- Panic/crash in tool handler when `thread_id` is set (or on created_ts formatting), bringing down HTTP server.

## Repro Steps
1. Start server: `cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail -- serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765`.
2. Call `send_message(project_key="/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust", sender_name="RusticGlen", to=["RusticGlen"], subject="...", body_md="...", thread_id="br-threadid-test")`.
3. Observe whether request completes; then call `health_check` / `fetch_inbox`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `send_message` returns promptly for trivial messages (no attachments) regardless of `thread_id`.
- Server remains responsive after `send_message`.
- Add a regression test (unit or conformance) that fails fast on handler stalls/panics.

 {"id":"br-2ei.2","title":"Storage: Git archive write/read pipeline + attachments","description":"Implement the git-backed mailbox archive described in EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md.

Legacy Python writes messages + file reservations + attachments into a per-project git repo and uses it as an auditable artifact store.

Scope (must match legacy behavior):
- Archive root creation + .gitattributes
- Per-project advisory locks (.archive.lock + owner metadata)
- Commit batching queue (max 10, max wait 50ms)
- Message write pipeline:
  - canonical messages/YYYY/MM/*.md
  - agents/<Agent>/inbox + outbox copies
  - thread digest files (messages/threads/<thread_id>.md) if present in legacy
  - git commit message format
- File reservation artifacts (file_reservations/<sha1(pattern)>.json + id-<id>.json)
- Attachment pipeline:
  - compute content hashes
  - convert images to WebP when enabled
  - inline small attachments (< 64KiB) into markdown
  - keep originals when configured
  - write manifests + audit logs
- Read helpers for resources that include commit metadata.

Constraints:
- Prefer git2 library; avoid shelling out when possible.
- Prefer asupersync for IO/concurrency.

## Success Criteria
1. Artifact conformance (br-2ei.1.3) can assert archive artifacts match legacy (layout + frontmatter + body + attachment manifests).
2. E2E archive suite (br-2ei.9.2) passes and produces rich artifacts/logs.
3. Benchmarks include archive write throughput and lock/commit batching overhead budgets.
4. No cross-process corruption: advisory locks are cross-platform and stale-lock recovery is safe.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:06:24.771240880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:32:27.969412199Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:32:27.969352095Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-2ei.2.2","title":"Archive: per-project advisory locks + commit batching queue","description":"Implement concurrency control for archive writes.

Spec:
- Per-project advisory lock file: <project>/.archive.lock (+ optional owner metadata JSON).
- Commit queue batching: max 10 commits or max wait 50ms.
- Stale lock cleanup on startup.

Implementation notes:
- Use filesystem lock (cross-platform) + busy-timeout retry. Prefer asupersync primitives if available.
- Keep API simple for callers: `with_project_lock(project_slug, || ...)`.
- Batching should preserve ordering for operations within a single process.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests simulate concurrent lock acquisition.
- Bench: lock acquisition overhead is low.

 {"id":"br-9jl","title":"CLI: share verify/decrypt parity (signature + age)","description":"## Objective
Implement `share verify` and `share decrypt` with legacy semantics.

## Scope
- `share verify`:
  - Validate SRI hashes and optional Ed25519 signature.
  - `--public-key` uses provided base64 key; else use manifest signature.
- `share decrypt`:
  - `--output` defaults to filename sans `.age` (or `_decrypted`).
  - `--identity` (age key file) is mutually exclusive with `--passphrase`.

## Tests
- Unit tests for signature verification and decrypt flag validation.
- Integration tests decrypting a bundle produced by share export.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store verification results under `tests/artifacts/cli/share_verify/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. share verify/decrypt match legacy flags, defaults, and exit codes.

 {"id":"br-ave","title":"Share: sqlite snapshot + project scope","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Steps 1–2: `create_sqlite_snapshot` and `apply_project_scope` with exact legacy semantics.

## Scope
- **create_sqlite_snapshot**:
  - Resolve destination path; create parent dirs.
  - Error if destination exists (never overwrite).
  - Open source DB; run `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(PASSIVE)` when enabled.
  - Use sqlite backup API to copy source → destination.
- **apply_project_scope**:
  - Load projects (id, slug, human_key); error if none.
  - If identifiers empty → no deletions.
  - Match identifiers case‑insensitive by slug or human_key; error on unknown.
  - Delete order per spec (agent_links, sibling_suggestions, messages + recipients, file_reservations, agents, projects).

## Tests
- Unit tests for identifier matching + error cases.
- Integration tests with temp DB and multiple projects; verify deletions and counts.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Save before/after row counts under `tests/artifacts/share/snapshot/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Snapshot creation refuses overwrite and produces byte‑identical DB backup.
2. Project scoping deletes only disallowed project data in the specified order.
3. +    use mcp_agent_mail_db::sqlmodel::Value as SqlValue;

+    let created_at_us = 1_704_067_200_000_000i64; // 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z

+
+    let conn = sqlmodel_sqlite::SqliteConnection::open_file(db_path.display().to_string())
+        .expect("open sqlite db");

+        .expect("init schema");
+
+    conn.execute_sync(
+        "INSERT INTO projects (id, slug, human_key, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
+        &[
+            SqlValue::BigInt(1),

+            SqlValue::Text(proj_alpha_key),

+        ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)";
+
+    // Recipient

+        &[
+            SqlValue::BigInt(1),
+            SqlValue::BigInt(1),
+            SqlValue::Text("GreenCastle".to_string()),
+            SqlValue::Text("test".to_string()),
+            SqlValue::Text("test".to_string()),
+            SqlValue::Text(String::new()),
+            SqlValue::BigInt(0),
+            SqlValue::BigInt(0),
+            SqlValue::Text("auto".to_string()),
+            SqlValue::Text("auto".to_string()),
+        ],

+    .unwrap();
+    // Sender
+    conn.execute_sync(
+        agent_insert,
+        &[
+            SqlValue::BigInt(2),
+            SqlValue::BigInt(1),
+            SqlValue::Text("PurpleBear".to_string()),
+            SqlValue::Text("test".to_string()),
+            SqlValue::Text("test".to_string()),
+            SqlValue::Text(String::new()),
+            SqlValue::BigInt(0),
+            SqlValue::BigInt(0),
+            SqlValue::Text("auto".to_string()),
+            SqlValue::Text("auto".to_string()),
+        ],
+    )
+    .unwrap();
+
+    // One ack-required message to exercise `acks` + `list-acks` queries.
+    let msg_id = 100i64;
+    conn.execute_sync(

+            ack_required, created_ts, attachments\
+        ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
+        &[
+            SqlValue::BigInt(msg_id),
+            SqlValue::BigInt(1),
+            SqlValue::BigInt(2),
+            SqlValue::Null,
+            SqlValue::Text("Ack needed".to_string()),
+            SqlValue::Text("please ack".to_string()),
+            SqlValue::Text("normal".to_string()),
+            SqlValue::BigInt(1),
+            SqlValue::BigInt(created_at_us + 10),
+            SqlValue::Text("[]".to_string()),
+        ],
+    )
+    .unwrap();
+
+    conn.execute_sync(
+        "INSERT INTO message_recipients (message_id, agent_id, kind) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
+        &[
+            SqlValue::BigInt(msg_id),
+            SqlValue::BigInt(1),
+            SqlValue::Text("to".to_string()),
+        ],
+    )
